All right, let's talk about the scheduling page. And as I mentioned in the overview video, we use Acuity. Now there are many tools for scheduling you can use. Many of them offer similar features. I've found this to have the most kind of customization features for what we needed to do. And so that's what we're choosing to use. That's what you're going to see here. But as you go through this, if you use something else, I'm really going to be pointing out optimizations of settings that will help make this a more fluid, great experience for your clients and pre-customers. So just think about how those could translate to your tool if you're going to stick with that. Or you can go through our step-by-step checklist for Acuity if you choose to use Acuity. So this is the scheduling page that someone is directed to. And this is really just set up and WordPress as a page that can be embedded. So if we look here, this is the actual raw Acuity scheduling page. We've just taken that appointment type, and we have embedded it into a WordPress page. So that's all that's going on here. I'm going to now take you through a few different settings behind the scenes. So if we look at the scheduling page link, we'll start there. This is where, using Acuity, you can get your links for schedule. This is also where you can get that embedding link. So there's a general scheduling link that will actually show all the different types of appointments that you have set up. But then what you can do is you can choose an appointment type. So for example, here's that remaining founder seven. I can get the direct link to that. Or I can get the embed scheduler, which is this code right here. So on the WordPress page that we were looking at, that's all it is. It's just this embedded iframe code that has been placed onto the WordPress page. So that's an important thing to know. The second thing I want to look at in this video is the appearance settings. So in most cases, you're going to want to set to daily. That's going to be the best kind of application funnel flow that you're going to want. You're not going to say I want to show them a full schedule. You're going to want to show them the days that you're going to be setting up in the next 72 hour-ish window. And so that is going to be a setting there. You can add your logo to give it a little bit more custom look if you'd like, your business name. And then this is probably the most important setting I wanted to show you here. I would highly recommend that you require clients to choose their time zone when booking. This will really cut down on confusion. If you don't do that, anyone that's not in your time zone may think that they're scheduling in their time zone. And you're going to end up with much more rescheduling and or cancellations missed appointments. So I would go ahead and set that. And that will be the most important thing to do there. Next, we'll look at appointment types if we click here. This is where you will set up the various types of appointments that you will be scheduling for with your clients or pre-customers. And so what I'm going to do here is I'm going to show you the one that we were looking at in the example. That's this remaining founder 7. Essentially, in a Q&A, you're just going to name it. You can put a simple description. And then the most important things are durations. So how long do you want this appointment to be scheduled for? We had these set to 45 minutes. If you want to buffer these, you can block off time afterwards. I always tend to do this. This allows me time to process and think about that call, follow up with any notes that I want to get into the CRM. It ensures that I don't get booked back to back. Now, this is not visible to the person scheduling with me. So this is just making sure that it doesn't block those calls in too close to one another. You can add a picture if you'd like. And then the other most important detail here is assigning team members. So if there are multiple people taking these types of calls, this is where you will set that. So you can see that both Ryan and I are set to be able to accept calls for this automated enrollment remaining founder 7 type call. And this will become important when I show you the next setting where you can choose how those calls are distributed. Next, we're going to look at availability. And there's two kind of slices that we need to think about here. So one is we have our team members, right? So you can see that I'm listed, Ryan is listed as well. And so what we're doing here essentially is we are choosing each individual team members availability relative to an appointment type. So while you can see there are different types of appointments, so I have a virtual team, marketing deep dives, these enrollment type calls. I don't necessarily want my availability to be the same for all of them. For example, I use time blocking strategies where certain hours are kind of held on my calendar for specific things, so some for sales, some for client engagements and fulfilling my responsibilities there. And so that's what this is really allowing. This is allowing me to say that these two calls, for example, are of the type, sales call or enrollment call. And so what do I want my personal availability to be for enrollment type calls? And so that's what you're seeing here. I can go in and edit my availability and set specific range of hours that I am willing and able to take those calls. Now, you'll see when we get into calendar integration, you can also synchronize to Google so it's looking at your real-time availability. But this kind of sits on top of it. And the reason for that is you can see here, for example, I am set to not take calls at present on Saturday on Sunday. Well, if you were to look at my Google Calendar, those days would appear completely free because I'm not booked for anything. So if I was not to layer this on top of that, that then acuity would allow calls to get scheduled on Saturday and Sunday. So that's an example of why you still use this, even if you're synchronizing with Google Calendar. And so each team member can set their own schedule. Ryan might say that he's willing to take calls on the weekend, for example. Looks like he's set those two off as well. But this allows each team member to set their individual availability. The other thing that this grouping does when you assign a specific appointment to a group, when we start to look at the notification setting, so the confirmation emails, the SMS texts that can go out, those templates are specific to group type. So that's the other reason you want to have your appointments set into various group types. Next, we're gonna look at integrations here. And there's just a few things I wanna call out for you. This is one of the reasons I like acuity. It has pretty good integrations across the board. There's a few things that are important here. This I need to reauthenticate. But if you are running ads at present and you want to be able to pixel visitors to specific scheduling pages, and you also wanna create custom audiences based on scheduling events, you can very easily integrate your pixel ID into acuity and then that will track those events on the Facebook ad platform. So very nice, easy feature there. Custom conversion tracking. I'm actually going to show you a bit of a hack here. And in the workbook or the notes, I will share this code with you. One of the things that I didn't love until I figured this trick out was that in acuity, when someone schedules, there wasn't a really easy way to redirect them to a custom confirmation page. And so you've seen this in our fireside chat where we've sent to a custom confirmation page with a video. By default, acuity just kind of pulls up this you've been scheduled page. Thank you, it's like an overlay on the schedule. We wanted to do something a bit fancier, which is what you saw in the kind of demo where we walked through our process. So a little trick here, if you go into this custom conversion tracking, I've basically done a little JavaScript thing here. And you can see where it's basically only looking at this appointment type. So if the appointment type is explore automated intimacy, then it's going to actually redirect to this AI booking confirmation page. And so that is just a very easy way to kind of override the normal setting within acuity and have that custom redirect. And so I will put this code into the workbook, but all you need to do is you need to edit this to have the URL of where you wish that redirect to go. And then you basically want to have the name of your appointment type. So that's what you can see there. Now you could have multiples of these. So if you have multiple types of different confirmations, you could have add additional scripts in here as well. But I have basically just this one sales funnel. So that's why you only see this type here. Next thing I want to show you, zoom integration, quite easy. You're just going to want to connect this to your zoom and then tell acuity which appointment types you want to add a zoom link to. So very easy. And then it basically here, it asks you if you have multiple users, which zoom account to sync with. So you can see both myself and Rai have our own zoom links or zoom accounts. And so it's basically just saying when someone schedules a call with me, make sure it uses fill zoom and vice versa. So this is really nice. And then in your email confirmations, it will include that zoom link when you use this location tag, which you'll see when we look at the email templates within acuity. And that is essentially all I am using at present within these integrations. Sync with other calendars. This is a good one to look at. So this is where you can authenticate your Google calendar to acuity. And then you can choose which calendar on your Google account you sync to for your availability. So this will make sure that new appointments that get scheduled get added to your Google calendar. It'll also make sure that you're blocking off time so that you're not getting double booked. And that's what this checkbox is here. Interesting thing here. If we look at that again, sorry. Rai is responsible for his. So if I was to select this, it's looking at my calendar. So that's why this is not set. He needs to set his within his actual acuity login. And the reason for this is that for privacy, each individual user who is synchronizing to the acuity account can choose how much of their visibility is being seen by other team members. So that's why that set that way. Next up, we're going to look at client emails. So this is where you set the kind of automated email that comes directly from acuity when someone schedules. And so you can set these different groups. And they call them templates. So you can see initial confirmation and initial confirmation too. And so each of these groups is going to get a different type of email. And so these up here, the initial, it's a very simple template. So you can see it's literally just saying appointment is scheduled for first name, last name. It's going to show who that appointment is scheduled with, how long it's scheduled for. And if you remember, when I showed you the Zoom integration, it's going to show that Zoom link as the location. And then it's got a few buttons here. So you can get clever with this if you'd like. This is kind of the basic template that acuity sets up for you. Now, when we look at these automated intimacy calls, which are enrollment calls, we'll see that we've set this up a bit different. So we've kept the top part the same, mostly because people are used to getting these. Acuity is a very commonly used scheduling tool. People are used to getting these. They're familiar. So we kind of left the top section the same. But down here, this is where we've put in our specific language that we want people to, just some additional conversations and different things we want to share. And I believe Rai's already walked through this language. But that's where you can do that. So you can see because they're in this confirmation to section, they're getting that language, which is very specific to automated intimacy. So that's what you want to do there. And then the last thing I'll show you is client SMS. So this is where you can decide if you want to send SMS reminders about your appointments, which I would highly recommend. So two things. Obviously, you have to capture their phone number on the application scheduling flow. And then you need to come in here and enable those SMS reminders. And then you're going to choose when those reminders go out. So we essentially have that set to one day before. They've updated this a little bit. You can add additional templates. So if you want to have something go, for example, an hour before, you could also choose to do that. This is where you can set up those settings. And then you also have choices on what information you want to show. And this will, you can see this update here. This will preview exactly what that text is going to look like. So you can see this is a very simple format here. If I tag this on, it'll show Empire Engineering. You can see the different settings there. So this is pretty simple. We keep it pretty simple. But this is where all the SMS settings lie. And that's pretty much it. Again, I will provide a checklist of all these various settings and where they live. I would just recommend going through, making sure you have those all set, because they do make a big difference. If you're not using Acuity, you've probably done a few ideas of what to look for in your scheduling tool. Or you could consider changing if you find that what you're currently using is a bit more limiting with your capabilities. Hope this helps. All right, talk to you next time.
